body:
As a general rule for homes in Pleasanton, you should have your garage door professionally serviced **once a year**.

Because a garage door is the largest moving part of your home—operating on a complex system of high-tension springs, cables, and rollers—annual maintenance is the industry standard to keep it running safely and prevent a sudden, costly breakdown. [Essential Garage Door Maintenance Tips for Pleasanton Homes](https://www.valiantdoor.com/blog/essential-garage-door-maintenance-tips-pleasanton-homes#:~:text=Why%20Regular%20Maintenance%20Matters,the%20chance%20of%20sudden%20breakdowns.)

However, your specific schedule might change depending on how you use your garage and the Tri-Valley climate.

### When to Service Every 6 to 9 Months

You may want to bump professional service up to twice a year (or every 6–9 months) if your home falls into any of these categories:

  * **High-Use Households:** If your garage is the main entryway for your home and cycles open and closed more than 4–6 times a day (busy families, multiple drivers, etc.), the hardware wears down much faster. [How Often Should You Service a Garage Door? A Realistic Schedule for CA Homes](https://localgaragedoors.com/2026/01/21/how-often-should-you-service-a-garage-door-a-realistic-schedule-for-ca-homes/#:~:text=More%20cycles%20means%20more%20friction,opener%20issues%20in%20the%20past.)

  * **Older Systems:** If your garage door or opener is more than 10 years old, the springs are nearing their cycle limits, and the parts require more frequent monitoring. [How Often Should You Service a Garage Door? A Realistic Schedule for CA Homes](https://localgaragedoors.com/2026/01/21/how-often-should-you-service-a-garage-door-a-realistic-schedule-for-ca-homes/#:~:text=5\)%20Older%20Doors%20\(10%2B,be%20serviced%20more%20often%20if%3A)

  * **Inland Heat Exposure:** Pleasanton summers can get quite hot. Intense heat and direct afternoon sun can dry out lubricants faster and cause metal components to expand and contract, which slightly shifts track alignment over time. [How Often Should You Service a Garage Door? A Realistic Schedule for CA Homes](https://localgaragedoors.com/2026/01/21/how-often-should-you-service-a-garage-door-a-realistic-schedule-for-ca-homes/#:~:text=Inland%20California%20heat%20can%20affect,every%206%E2%80%939%20months%20if%3A)

### Quick DIY Checks You Can Do Monthly

Between professional visits, you can keep an eye on things yourself with a quick 5-minute check:

  * **The Eye & Ear Test:** Watch the door move. Does it jerk, sag, or move unevenly? Listen for any new grinding, squeaking, or popping sounds.

  * **Safety Sensor Test:** Wave a broom handle under the door while it’s closing to ensure the photo-eye sensors instantly trigger the door to reverse.

  * **Balance Test:** Pull the emergency release cord to operate the door manually. Lift it halfway up and let go. If it stays in place, it’s balanced. If it crashes down or shoots upward, the spring tension needs professional adjustment.

> ⚠️ **A Quick Safety Note:** If you notice frayed cables or a gap in your torsion spring, **do not attempt to fix or tighten them yourself.** Those components are under immense pressure and can cause serious injury. Always leave spring and cable adjustments to a professional.

How old is your current garage door system, and has it started making any unusual noises lately?
